one_liner: East African spice blend for pilau rice - cumin, cardamom, black pepper, cloves, sometimes garam masala and paprika
twist_idea: Market as 'One-Pot Rice Seasoning' targeting meal prep crowd - position against Zatarain's with more complex flavor, subscription sachets

one_liner: Kenyan coconut curry spice blend, milder and creamier than Indian curry, coconut-forward
twist_idea: Brand as 'Coconut Curry Simmer Sauce Base' - just add coconut milk, target weeknight dinner crowd tired of Thai curry packets

one_liner: Roasted blend of ginger, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, black pepper, nutmeg for East African chai
twist_idea: Market as 'Spiced Latte Base' for oat milk crowd - position as cleaner/more authentic than Starbucks chai concentrate, in glass jars

one_liner: Kenyan breakfast porridge blend - roasted soy, amaranth, sorghum, millet, cassava, cardamom, cinnamon - low GI whole grain
twist_idea: Rebrand as 'Ancient Grain Breakfast Bowl' targeting paleo/whole30 crowd - sachets with add-hot-water convenience, vs. instant oatmeal

one_liner: Kenyan root vegetable porridge flour - cassava, arrowroot, sweet potato blended with peanuts, nutrient-dense filling breakfast
twist_idea: Market as 'Root Fuel Breakfast Blend' for gym/CrossFit crowd - high-carb pre-workout meal, emphasize cassava as clean carb source

one_liner: 100% natural Kenyan all-purpose seasoning, FDA certified, no preservatives
twist_idea: Rebrand with modern packaging as 'Savanna Spice' - target clean-label crowd, position against Mrs. Dash with more exotic profile

one_liner: Hard white corn (not soft yellow US corn) used for githeri - bean and corn stew, chewier texture and earthier taste
twist_idea: Market as 'Heritage White Corn Grits' targeting Southern US + farm-to-table restaurants - emphasize heirloom variety, stone-ground

one_liner: Kenyan stew/gravy seasoning blend, used for meat and vegetable mchuzi (sauce/stew)
twist_idea: Rebrand as 'One-Pot Stew Magic' - target Instant Pot crowd, position as base for dump-and-go meals

one_liner: Tanzanian version of pilau spice - slightly different ratio than Kenyan, more coastal Swahili influence
twist_idea: Market as 'Coastal Rice Spice' emphasizing seafood pairing - target home cooks who want restaurant-quality rice sides

one_liner: Ethiopian chili-spice blend - complex, earthy, slightly sweet heat with fenugreek, cardamom, coriander, paprika base
twist_idea: Market as 'Fire Spice Rub' for grilling season - target BBQ enthusiasts looking beyond standard rubs, emphasize coffee pairing notes

one_liner: South African vegetable relish/stew spice - sweet, tangy, slightly spicy, tomato-forward
twist_idea: Market as 'Veggie Bowl Seasoning' targeting plant-based crowd - position as the spice that makes vegetables exciting

one_liner: African bird's eye chili blend, citrusy heat - the original peri peri before Nando's
twist_idea: Market as 'OG Peri Peri' - compete with Nando's bottled sauce by offering the pure spice blend for home cooks, hot sauce kit angle

one_liner: Salt from Kalahari Desert, naturally smoked, mineral-rich with unique terroir
twist_idea: Market as 'Desert Smoke Salt' in premium grinder - target finishing salt market dominated by Maldon, emphasize African origin story

one_liner: South African curry blend from Durban's Indian community - warmer, slightly sweeter than UK curry powder
twist_idea: Market as 'Bunny Chow Curry Mix' with recipe for iconic Durban street food - target food blogger/viral recipe crowd

one_liner: African herb blend inspired by wild savanna herbs - earthy, slightly gamey, pairs with red meat
twist_idea: Market as 'Wild Game Rub' for hunters and venison/bison eaters - position as the missing seasoning for lean wild meats

product_name: Supu ya Ndizi (Plantain Flour Soup Mix)
one_liner: Green plantain flour used for thick savory soups in coastal Kenya/Tanzania, naturally gluten-free thickener
twist_idea: Market as 'Plantain Thickener' for gluten-free cooking - position against cornstarch/arrowroot with added nutrition and unique flavor

one_liner: Finger millet flour for traditional Kenyan porridge - high calcium, iron, amino acids, naturally gluten-free
twist_idea: Market as 'Millet Morning Bowl' targeting ancient grain trend - position as the next quinoa, emphasize African origin over Indian

one_liner: Kenyan bean stew spice blend - cumin, coriander, turmeric, ginger, garlic powder - makes red kidney beans creamy and flavorful
twist_idea: Market as 'Bean Magic Seasoning' for vegetarians/meal preppers - sachets that turn canned beans into a complete meal, vs. bland beans

one_liner: Spice blend for Kenyan-style masala fries - tangy, spicy, slightly sweet with ketchup-chili-lemon base
twist_idea: Market as 'Fry Dust' targeting late-night snackers and fast-food hackers - TikTok viral angle, works on air fryer fries

one_liner: Kenyan fermented milk culture, traditionally made in gourds with special charcoal - probiotic-rich, tangy, slightly smoky
twist_idea: Market as 'Smokehouse Kefir Culture' for home fermenting crowd - position as more interesting than yogurt culture, DIY probiotic kit
